# config

<!-- awf:edit current-state: from .awf/domains/parts/config/current-state.md -->
## Current state

The `currentState` block configures topic validation and is the authority the current-state model reads: strict `sources` (each with `globs`, a `marker` token, and an optional `close`) and `testGlobs`, `topicCoverage` and `topicFanout` severities (default `error` and `warn`), and a positive `maxTopicsPerPath` budget (default 8 without replacing an explicitly configured value). It replaced the legacy `invariants` block, which the project-atomic cutover dropped. Topic inputs live in paired fixed trees: strict sidecars at `.awf/topics/metadata/<domain>/<topic>.yaml` and constrained Markdown at `.awf/topics/parts/<domain>/<topic>/current-state.md`. The closed-tree sweep claims only valid pairs and their structural directories; orphan halves, unknown files, invalid path components, and unconfigured domain directories fail.

First adoption seals initializedWithVersion plus both ADR format cutoffs and gaps. Empty repositories use both cutoffs at 1; brownfield repositories use highest-plus-one for both with every missing lower identity recorded. Upgrading an existing V1 adopter preserves its V1 cutoff and seals `adrFormatV2From` at highest-plus-one in the same atomic schema-15 lock save. Ordinary sync and forced re-init preserve that authority.

The permanent `.awf/awf.lock` carries both ADR format cutoffs (`adrFormatV1From`, `adrFormatV2From`) and an explicit non-null legacy ADR gap array (`legacyAdrGaps`, including `[]`) promoted at the cutover, and every later sync preserves all three authority fields while regenerating the file manifest. Lock validation rejects a missing or null permanent gap set, invalid semantic-version provenance, and a bridge boundary whose cutoff or sorted unique positive gaps do not define a valid closed legacy set; a transient `bridgeAttestation` block (format version, clean prepared HEAD, and post-normalization tree digest) appears only between the preceding bridge release's attestation and this binary's consumption of it, and is cleared when the cutover completes. `.awf/current-state-upgrade.journal` is the durable transaction record the closed-tree sweep claims during a cutover, so a present journal is recognized transaction state, never an orphan; it is removed when recovery finishes.

Schema generation 15 adds the one-save `adr-format-v2-cutoff` migration without rewriting authored ADRs. The 0.20.0 binary owns this generation and selects V2 scaffolding at and above the sealed cutoff.

ADR-0132 adds schema generation 13 and the `exploring-skill-closure` migration. It reuses close-enabled-set to add `exploring` automatically when an adopted config enables brainstorming, debugging, or refactor-coupling-audit.

ADR-0127 retires the `audit.baseBranch` key: a schema-11 migration strips it from `.awf/config.yaml` and announces the removal, and `config.RemoveMappingKey` is the new nested-key remover it needs (the existing `RemoveKey` walks only top-level entries, so it silently no-ops on a nested key).

Per-project configuration lives in a `.awf/` tree (relocated out of the runtime's `.claude/` directory by ADR-0016, schema migration `{To:3}`): a skeleton `config.yaml` (prefix, vars, flat enable arrays for skills/agents/docs/domains, the adapter `targets` array, and the `currentState` block) plus per-artifact sidecars and convention parts. The config is strict-parsed (`KnownFields`), and drift is tracked in a schema-versioned `awf.lock`. Schema migrations are an ordered registry applied by `awf upgrade`; `awf sync`/`check` gate a stale layout (a pre-relocation `.claude/awf/` tree is detected and gated until upgraded). Schema migration `{To:4}` dropped the `hooks:` enable array with the hook kind (ADR-0032). Schema migration `{To:5}` added the `bootstrap` enable entry (a nested `bootstrap.enabled` scalar rather than a top-level array) with the self-pinning bootstrap (ADR-0040). ADR-0048 reintroduced the `hooks:` key as a nested `hooks.enabled` mapping for the rendered git-hook payloads (parse-safe because the schema-4 migration strips only the legacy array shape) with no accompanying migration: `awf init` scaffolds it enabled, existing adopters opt in via `awf enable hooks`. An additive optional field is backward-safe only where the parser tolerates it: a new `config.yaml` key an old strict parser never sees is fine, but the sidecar `paths` field (ADR-0077) shipped under the schema-7 generation bump precisely because sidecars are strict-parsed too: an old binary rejects a sidecar carrying the new key, and the version gate keeps it away. Schema migration `{To:7}` (ADR-0077) ports every no-slash pattern in `invariants.sources[].globs` and `audit.dependencyManifests` to the anchored `**/<pattern>` form via `config.AnchorNoSlashGlobs`, the nested-sequence editor beside the scalar ones. The `config.yaml` that `awf init` scaffolds enables a curated workflow-core set (ADR-0022), only the catalog's `core`-flagged skills, plus all agents, while seeding every template-referenced var (across all template families, including the always-on plain singletons) so a later opt-in `awf enable` renders cleanly. No doc carries `core` any longer: the three docs that used to (`workflow`, `doc-standard`, `agents-md-standard`) are mandatory singletons outside the toggleable `docs:` catalog (ADR-0043), and a schema migration (`{To: 6}`) relocates their sidecar/convention-part paths and strips them from an upgrading project's `docs:` array. Those fillable values are declared as descriptors in the catalog's `vars:` block (ADR-0029), `{key, kind, description, default, options, target}`, and `ScaffoldConfig` takes the resolved var values (plus the catalog trim and audit scopes below), so a populated `config.yaml` can be written at init rather than only blank vars; it seeds no `currentState` block; that section is authored post-init. The `vars:` block also carries two `multiselect` catalog-trim descriptors (`skills`/`docs`, `target: catalog-skills`/`catalog-docs`) whose options derive from the catalog; `ScaffoldConfig` takes an optional `*config.CatalogTrim` that replaces the core skills/docs enable arrays verbatim when supplied (nil keeps the curated core). The four catalog/domain enable arrays are managed through `awf enable`/`disable <kind> <name>` and surfaced by `awf list` (ADR-0024); the editor is block-scoped, so a name shared across kinds (e.g. a `debugging` skill and doc) is toggled in the right array only. The adapter `targets` array has a parallel bespoke `awf enable/disable/list target` path (ADR-0037); it is not a catalog kind, so it stays out of the kind-descriptor machinery and refuses disabling the last target. `config.yaml` is constructed and mutated only through `internal/config` (ADR-0026): `MarshalSkeleton` emits the scaffold, `SetArrayMember` edits one array member, `SetArray` (ADR-0037) writes a whole computed list, and `SetMappingScalar` (ADR-0040) writes a nested `bootstrap.enabled` bool (so adding `cursor` to an absent, defaulted `targets` key materializes `[claude, cursor]` rather than dropping the default) all behind a single two-space `encode` funnel that retires the former hand-rolled emitter and string editor (`internal/migrate`'s forward-compat marshalling excepted). The `skills`/`agents` enable arrays may also carry project-local names absent from the catalog (ADR-0068): such a name must be declared by a `.awf/<kind>/<name>.yaml` sidecar (its `data.description` feeding the rendered frontmatter) and pass `config.ValidateArtifactName`, which enforces kebab-case so a name cannot smuggle path or template metacharacters; `awf new skill|agent` scaffolds exactly that declaration. Sidecar `data:` keys are now per-key overrides over catalog defaults, an absent key falls through, a present key (even null or empty) suppresses the default (ADR-0045), and the skills enable array feeds the render context and the config hash of `.skills`-referencing templates, so toggling a skill reflags dependent artifacts (ADR-0046); no schema bump, the tree's shape is unchanged. Reviewing skills are pair-validated with the reviewer agents they dispatch (ADR-0050): a catalog `requiresAgent` on each `reviewing-*` spec fails every gated command at project open when an enabled, non-local reviewing skill's agent is missing from the `agents:` array, `awf disable agent` refuses upfront while an enabled skill requires the agent, and `awf enable skill` enables a missing required agent in the same config rewrite. Commit scopes have one storage (ADR-0051): the `commitScope` var is gone from the catalog; a `commitScopes` init descriptor (`target: audit-scopes`) routes a comma-separated answer into `audit.allowedScopes`, which `awf commit-gate`/`awf audit` and the rendered reviewing-skill prose both consume through `audit.Resolve`, and `awf init` writes the block only for a non-empty answer (nil = accept any). The lock and every migration rewrite of an existing config file are written atomically, a same-directory temp file renamed into place (`manifest.WriteFileAtomic`, ADR-0076), so an interrupted process can no longer truncate a trust-bearing file; and a present-but-unreadable `awf.lock` is a hard error in every reader via the `manifest.LoadOptional` choke point (one recovery hint: restore from version control or delete deliberately to re-adopt), with a missing lock keeping each caller's no-lock semantics. The enabled set is closure-validated at project open (ADR-0081): every enabled, non-`local` artifact's direct `requires*` requirements must be enabled (the ADR-0050 agent pairing became one edge of that general check) and schema migration `{To:8}` (`close-enabled-set`) closes an upgrading config, adding missing skill/agent/doc requirements and dropping dormant doc-gated skills; `awf init` closure-completes a trimmed selection and derives its agents from the trim. The var-descriptor policy is narrowed by ADR-0084: a descriptor exists only for a functional value (a command, an enforced identifier set, or a structural path) never to tune prose wording (the convention-part mechanism is the prose-customization path); the four single-consumer prose knobs (`docCurrencyTargets`, `adrProposeCommitFmt`, `gateDuration`, `modulePrefix`) are removed with their template fallbacks promoted to unconditional prose, and the surviving eight-key string descriptor set is pinned by a test (`invariant: var-descriptor-set-pinned`) so any extension is a successor-ADR act; a removed key in a saved init answers file hard-errors on a fresh `awf init`. ADR-0086 closes the tree's residue holes: `awf check` fails on any `.awf/` entry outside the claimed-path model (sidecars and declared-section parts of enabled artifacts, the rendered units, the skeleton; `memory/**` exempt, sync-written `*.awf-bak` backups flagged with a stale-backup detail, a `local: true` artifact's parts unclaimed), and on authored-but-unconsumed configuration: a non-empty `vars:` key no rendered artifact references (so leftover adopter keys are no longer inert once valued; empty seeds stay legal as the seeded open-to-do state, ADR-0022, nudged by the unset-var note per ADR-0087) or a sidecar `data:` key the artifact's templates never read. Inert sidecar fields refuse at project open: `paths:` outside a domain sidecar, and anything but `paths:` on a domain sidecar (`data:`, `sections:`, `local: true`; a domain sidecar is paths-only). Interactive `awf init` prompts only for vars the scaffolded selection's templates reference; the full-union empty seeding is unchanged. Var keys carry three states since ADR-0087: present-and-set (configured), present-but-empty-or-null (the seeded open to-do the unset-var note nudges), and absent (the deliberate, git-auditable decline: deleting the key permanently acknowledges the note; expect a one-time stale flag until the next sync, since referenced vars fold into the config hash). Absence stays a reliable deletion signal by convention: a release that adds a catalog var descriptor ships a one-time schema-migration seed (`<key>: ""` where absent, run-once via the generation gate, so deletion is never resurrected), and `awf new skill|agent` seeds its base template's referenced vars at creation through `config.SeedVarKey`, the string sibling of `SetMappingScalar` that never touches a present key. ADR-0088 gives the whole configuration surface an adopter-facing description authority: `internal/configspec` describes every config key, sidecar field, var (derived verbatim from the catalog descriptors), and per-artifact data key, with reflection/template parity tests making an undescribed key a gate failure; the always-on generated `docs/config-reference.md` projects it per-project with live state (three-way var states per the deletion-as-acknowledgement model, consumers, dormant hints), and `awf config [<key-or-var>]` serves it from the CLI, gated inside an adopted tree, degrading to a static catalog reference outside one. The config-reference sidecar is sections/local-only: `data:` refuses at open because the reference's data namespace is injected at generation. ADR-0101 adds a third config-tree singleton toggle beside `bootstrap`/`hooks`: a nested `runner.enabled` mapping (nil or false = do not render, additive and default-off, no schema bump) gating the co-owned command-runner `x`; awf-the-repo leaves it disabled, and an adopter opts in via `awf enable runner` (or by setting the key); the runner joins `bootstrap`/`hooks` as a nameless-singleton CLI toggle.

ADR-0091 lets the `docs:` array carry project-local names absent from the catalog, the doc analogue of ADR-0068's skills/agents: such a name is declared by a `.awf/docs/<name>.yaml` sidecar (its `data.title`/`data.description` feeding the rendered doc and its document-map entry) and validated by a new path-aware `config.ValidateDocName` beside `ValidateArtifactName` (one or more lowercase-kebab `/`-separated segments, each carrying at least one letter or digit, rejecting a path escape, a leading/trailing/empty segment, a `.md` suffix, and the reserved `_base` stem) while skill and agent names stay flat. `awf new doc` scaffolds exactly that declaration; the base doc template's `title`/`description` data keys join the `internal/configspec` description authority. ADR-0103 adds a top-level `tags:` vocabulary (a governed map of cross-cutting keyword tag to one-line meaning, described in `internal/configspec` like any other key) against which `awf check` validates every ADR and pitfall tag (an empty or absent vocabulary is inert; a member with an empty meaning is drift; an unused member is allowed); it feeds no rendered artifact, so it stays out of the config hash and needs no schema migration. ADR-0119 adds a fourth config-tree block beside `bootstrap`/`hooks`/`runner`: `proseGate`, an additive default-off mapping (no schema migration) carrying `proseGate.enabled` and a `proseGate.exemptions` list of `{path, codepoint, count}` entries, each described in `internal/configspec` (the codepoint spelled `U+2014` rather than typed, so `config.yaml` does not trip the scan it configures) and projected into the config reference with a live-state entry count.

`currentState.sources[].close` (ADR-0121) is a literal close
token for block-comment markers, additive with no schema-generation bump (the `testGlobs`
precedent) - empty or absent means no stripping. Migrations validate serialized adopter data before destructive cleanup.
